_comment: Copy this file per-project (e.g. assets/selector-map.json), fill in real selectors, delete this comment key. One config drives both inject_microdata.js and generate_json_ld.js.

_property_fields.selector: CSS selector, scoped within the parent item's element, pointing at the node whose text/attribute IS the value
_property_fields.source: 'text' (textContent) or 'attribute' (needs 'attribute' field naming which one, e.g. 'src' or 'href')
_property_fields.value_pattern: optional regex; if the selected text contains extra formatting (e.g. a currency symbol) that isn't part of the actual value, this extracts just the matching portion. generate_json_ld.js applies it directly to the emitted value. inject_microdata.js cannot silently 'clean' visible DOM text a crawler will read verbatim -- if the full selected text doesn't equal the pattern match, it leaves the visible element untouched and adds a sibling <meta itemprop=...> with the cleaned value instead.
_property_fields.constant: a fixed value not derived from any visible element (e.g. priceCurrency, availability) -- always emitted as a meta/link child rather than tagging visible text
_property_fields.nestedItem: name of another entry in 'items' to recurse into for a nested schema.org type (e.g. Product.offers -> Offer)
